steps.title: Identify what the example must show
steps.content: The passage emphasizes “within-species variations” — changes in population size for a single species over time, not comparisons between different species. The example must illustrate one species fluctuating significantly.

steps.title: Match the graph data to this requirement
steps.content: Reading the graph, the Indo-Pacific sergeant population starts at approximately 2 individuals in January 2001 and reaches 28 in October 2001 — a dramatic shift within the same species across the months. Choice B states exactly this range and directly demonstrates the within-species variation the passage describes. Choices A and C misread the graph data (striated rockskipper and Cocos frillgoby never fluctuate as described), while choice D uses “whereas” to contrast rockskipper against sergeant, which illustrates between-species differences, not within-species variation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
